calls.summary: Cameron T was frustrated about a scheduling mix-up. Ava apologized, noted the details, and escalated the issue to the team for a callback.

transcript.text: Acme Clinic, this is Ava. How can I help?
My appointment wasn't in the system when I showed up.
I'm really sorry about that, Cameron. I'll flag this for the team right away so we can make it right.
Please do, that was frustrating.
Understood — someone will call you back shortly to sort it out. Thank you for your patience.

calls.summary: Noel N called Acme Clinic to book a first wellness session. Ava answered questions about availability and confirmed an appointment for later in the week.

transcript.text: Thank you for calling Acme Clinic, this is Ava. How can I help today?
Hi, I'd like to book my first session.
Wonderful — I can help with that. Have you visited us before, or is this your first time?
First time.
Great, welcome! We have openings Thursday morning. Would 10 AM work, Noel?
Thursday at 10 works perfectly.
You're all set. You'll get a text confirmation shortly. See you Thursday!

calls.summary: Wren C called after hours. Ava shared the next available opening and offered to text a booking link.

transcript.text: You've reached Acme Clinic. This is Ava — how can I help?
Are you open right now?
We're closed for the evening, but I can text you a link to book online. Would that help?
Sure, text me the link.
Sent! Have a good night.

calls.summary: Rowan A asked about pricing and hours at Acme Clinic. Ava shared the current package options and weekday availability.

transcript.text: Thanks for calling Acme Clinic, this is Ava. How can I help?
What are your hours and how much is a session?
We're open weekdays 9 to 5. Single sessions and monthly packages are available — would you like me to text you the details?
Yes, please text me.
Done — it's on the way. Anything else I can help with?
No, that's all. Thanks.
